# js-vtt

A TypeScript library (v1.0.4-beta) for parsing, building, and manipulating WebVTT subtitle files.
Published as an ESM-only npm package.

## What it does

- Parse `.vtt` strings, URLs, or JSON into a typed `VTT` object
- Build WebVTT files programmatically via a fluent/chainable API
- Serialize back to WebVTT or SRT format
- Manipulate cue timings: shift, rescale, and sync frame rates
- Validate the structure of a VTT file

## Tech stack

| Tool            | Purpose                                      |
|-----------------|----------------------------------------------|
| TypeScript 6    | Primary language; strict, ESM                |
| pnpm            | Package manager                              |
| tsdown          | Library bundler (outputs ESM + `.d.mts`)     |
| Vitest          | Unit testing + coverage via @vitest/coverage-v8 |
| ESLint 10       | Linting (typescript-eslint + prettier rules) |
| Prettier 3      | Code formatting                              |
| VitePress       | Documentation site (under docs/)            |
| changesets      | Versioning and changelog management          |
| tsx             | Dev/watch runner                             |
| lodash-es       | Only runtime dependency                      |

## Project structure

```
src/
  index.ts             — Public entry point (re-exports everything)
  vtt.ts               — Core VTT class
  helpers.ts           — Regex-based segment type detectors
  main.ts              — Dev scratch file
  segments/
    segment.ts         — Abstract base class for all segments
    cue.ts             — Timed text cue (startTime, endTime, text, settings)
    header.ts          — WEBVTT header with optional description and metadata
    region.ts          — REGION block (id, width, lines, anchors, scroll)
    style.ts           — STYLE block (CSS selectors + declarations)
    comment.ts         — NOTE (comment) block
    index.ts           — Re-exports all segment classes
  errors/
    CalledItWrongError.ts
    InvalidCommentError.ts
    InvalidCueError.ts
    InvalidHeaderError.ts
    InvalidRegionError.ts
    InvalidStyleError.ts
    InvalidVttError.ts
    SrtValidationError.ts
docs/
  index.md
  guide/getting-started.md
```

## Core API

### VTT class (`src/vtt.ts`)

```ts
// Construction
const vtt = new VTT(description?, meta?)

// Fluent builders (all return `this` for chaining)
vtt.setHeader(description?, meta?)
vtt.addCue(startTime, endTime, text, identifier?, settings?)
vtt.addRegion(id?, width?, lines?, regionAnchor?, viewportAnchor?, scroll?)
vtt.addStyle(selectors, declarations)
vtt.addComment(text)
vtt.addSegment(segment)  // generic; accepts any Segment instance

// Timing manipulation
vtt.shiftTime(offsetSeconds)          // shift all cues by ±offset
vtt.rescale(originalDuration, newDuration)  // scale timings proportionally
vtt.syncFps(sourceFps, targetFps)     // remap timings across frame rates

// Validation & serialization
vtt.validate()              // returns boolean
vtt.toJSON()                // returns plain object
vtt.toString('vtt' | 'srt') // default 'vtt'

// Static parsers
VTT.fromString(str)         // parse WebVTT string
VTT.fromURL(url)            // fetch and parse (returns Promise<VTT>)
VTT.fromJSON(json)          // reconstruct from toJSON() output
```

### Segment hierarchy

```
Segment (abstract)
  ├── Header   — WEBVTT line + optional description/metadata
  ├── Cue      — timed subtitle block; supports VTT and SRT output
  ├── Region   — positional region definition
  ├── Style    — embedded CSS block
  └── Comment  — NOTE block (stripped in SRT output)
```

All segments implement:
- `toString(format?, index?)` — serialise to VTT or SRT string
- `toJSON()` — plain-object representation
- `isValid()` — structural validation
- `static fromString(str)` — parse from raw block string

### CueSettings type

```ts
type CueSettings = {
  vertical?: 'rl' | 'lr';
  line?: number | string;
  position?: string;
  size?: string;
  align?: 'start' | 'center' | 'end';
  region?: string;
}
```

## Conventions

- All source files use `.ts` extensions internally but import with `.js` extensions (Node ESM resolution)
- Private class fields use the `#field` syntax
- All public mutators return `this` to enable method chaining
- Errors are typed custom classes in `src/errors/`
- No CommonJS; ESM-only (`"type": "module"` in package.json)
- Tests live alongside or near the source files and are run with `pnpm test`

## Scripts

```
pnpm dev           # watch mode via tsx
pnpm build         # bundle with tsdown
pnpm test          # run vitest with coverage
pnpm lint          # eslint
pnpm format        # prettier
pnpm docs:dev      # vitepress dev server
pnpm changeset     # create a changeset
pnpm release       # publish via changesets
```
